behaviors.description: Required patient fields (name, date of birth, insurance ID) are validated before any downstream processing and missing fields produce a clear error listing all missing items.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: validation must block downstream insurance/triage/scheduling calls and aggregate all missing fields. MOCK-MISS: a unit test of only the validator logic can pass while orchestration still invokes downstream modules on invalid input. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (>Unit): unit would miss a controller bug that builds the error correctly but still calls insurance service. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess is required for this behavior text; no AI output dependence; no multi-agentic chain, so Integration is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Run intake orchestrator with missing `name` and `insurance_id`, using in-process fakes for downstream services. Assert: (1) returned error lists both missing fields, (2) insurance/triage/scheduling/confirm fakes were not called.

behaviors.description: Date of birth validation rejects future dates, dates more than 150 years ago, and non-date strings.
behaviors.justification: This is local validation logic (parsing/range checks) within one function/object boundary. No module interaction is required to expose the described failures. REJECTION-GATE: higher levels were rejected because behavior text is only date-rule correctness, with no infrastructure or cross-module handoff requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven unit test for DOB validator with inputs: future date, 151-years-ago date, malformed string, and valid boundary values; assert specific rejection/acceptance outcomes.

behaviors.description: The insurance eligibility check sends the correct patient ID and plan code to the payer API and correctly interprets `eligible` vs `not eligible` vs `pending`.
behaviors.justification: This is API-client wiring plus response mapping across modules. MOCK-MISS: unit-testing only a mapper or request builder misses swapped field wiring at the service→client boundary. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (>Unit): unit would miss a bug where orchestration passes internal `patient_uuid` instead of payer `patient_id`. REJECTION-GATE for System: behavior text targets payload correctness and status interpretation, which an in-process fake API client can verify without real network conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test of eligibility service with fake payer client capturing request payload and returning each status. Assert outgoing `patient_id`/`plan_code` values and internal mapping to `eligible`/`not eligible`/`pending`.

behaviors.description: When the payer API is unreachable, the system marks eligibility as `pending` and continues the intake rather than blocking.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real HTTP/network failure modes (timeouts/connection errors) and continuation behavior under those errors. MOCK-HIDE: mocked exceptions may not match real transport error classes or timeout semantics, causing false confidence. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (>Integration): integration with in-process fakes would miss a real client-library timeout that is uncaught and aborts intake. INTEGRATION-BRAKE(a): yes, behavior explicitly references unreachable external API, so System is required.
behaviors.test_description: System test with payer endpoint intentionally unreachable (e.g., dead host/port timeout). Execute intake end-to-end up through scheduling trigger. Assert eligibility becomes `pending` and pipeline proceeds to subsequent steps instead of failing fast.

behaviors.description: Triage invocation includes the reported symptoms and configured emergency rubric in the model input payload.
behaviors.justification: This validates prompt/payload assembly across config + request modules, not model judgment quality. MOCK-MISS: unit-testing only a prompt template function can miss orchestrator dropping rubric config during call construction. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (>Unit): unit would miss integration bug where symptoms are passed but rubric key is omitted by caller. REJECTION-GATE for Agentic: behavior text is about input payload inclusion, not correctness of model output.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test with stub model client recording request body. Run triage call with known symptoms and rubric config; assert both are present and correctly serialized in the outbound payload.

behaviors.description: The live AI symptom triage assigns high urgency to symptom combinations that indicate emergency conditions (for example chest pain plus shortness of breath).
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model decision quality/non-determinism for emergency symptom combinations. MOCK-HIDE: deterministic stubs always return expected urgency and cannot detect real misclassification. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (>Integration): integration with canned responses would miss regression where live model outputs medium urgency for chest pain + shortness of breath. REJECTION-GATE for Workflow: behavior requires one model decision, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Agentic test suite calling live triage model on emergency symptom cases across repeated runs; assert high-urgency rate meets threshold and flag unsafe downgrades.

behaviors.description: The triage result parser stores a clinician-readable explanation field (not only a numeric score).
behaviors.justification: Behavior is parser + persistence handoff: explanation must survive transformation and storage. MOCK-MISS: a unit parser test can pass while repository mapping drops the explanation column/field. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (>Unit): unit misses bug where parsed explanation exists in memory but is not persisted. REJECTION-GATE for Agentic: behavior text checks field presence/storage, not quality of explanation content.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test feeding a fixed triage response containing `urgency_score` and `explanation`; run parser + save flow and assert stored record contains non-empty clinician-readable explanation.

behaviors.description: The live AI symptom triage explanation cites case-specific symptoms and urgency rationale, not generic boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: This is output-quality evaluation of live model reasoning text. MOCK-HIDE: stubbed explanations can be made specific by construction and cannot reveal real generic/boilerplate drift. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (>Integration): integration with fixtures misses live-model responses that ignore input symptoms and produce template text. REJECTION-GATE for Workflow: single triage generation quality check, not multi-agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Agentic evaluation on live triage outputs for varied symptom inputs; assert explanation references input-specific symptoms and contains explicit urgency rationale (with rubric-based scoring criteria).

behaviors.description: An appointment is scheduled in the first available slot that matches the assigned urgency level: high urgency gets same-day, medium gets within 48 hours.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is scheduler logic + calendar DB query/update interaction. MOCK-MISS: unit-testing slot selection alone misses mismatch between urgency policy and repository query ordering/filters. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (>Unit): unit misses bug where same-day filter is correct in code but DB query sorts by provider then time, skipping earliest valid slot. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no required real OS/network/subprocess per behavior text; no AI output quality check.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test with seeded calendar DB and urgency inputs (`high`, `medium`). Assert selected slot is first valid slot and satisfies same-day / <=48h constraints.

behaviors.description: The scheduling step does not double-book a time slot even when two patients are processed concurrently.
behaviors.justification: This is concurrent module+DB transaction behavior. Real failure appears at scheduler↔DB locking/constraint boundary, not single-function logic. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (>Unit): unit misses race where both threads read slot as free before either commit. REJECTION-GATE for System: behavior can be reproduced with in-process concurrent workers against real local DB transaction semantics; no external OS/network/subprocess dependency beyond local integration setup.
behaviors.test_description: Integration concurrency test: launch two intake scheduling calls simultaneously for the same slot against shared calendar DB with real constraints/transactions; assert only one booking succeeds and no duplicate row exists.

behaviors.description: The confirmation message includes patient name, appointment time, provider name, and preparation instructions appropriate to visit type.
behaviors.justification: Behavior spans data handoff (patient/schedule/provider/visit-type) into message composition. MOCK-MISS: unit test of template rendering misses upstream mapping bug that passes wrong provider or omits prep instructions. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (>Unit): unit misses integration defect where visit type mapping key differs (`visitType` vs `visit_type`) so prep instructions are blank. REJECTION-GATE for higher levels: no real infra/LLM dependence in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test composing confirmation from realistic intake+scheduling objects; assert message contains required fields and visit-type-specific preparation instructions.

behaviors.description: Pipeline orchestration (validate, insurance, triage, schedule, confirm) completes correctly with deterministic stubbed triage output.
behaviors.justification: Explicitly deterministic stubbed triage disqualifies agentic dependence; behavior targets orchestration and state handoff across modules. MOCK-MISS: unit tests of each step miss cross-step state propagation and branching defects. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (>Unit): unit misses bug where eligibility `pending` is not forwarded to scheduling context, breaking downstream confirm payload. REJECTION-GATE for System/Agentic: text explicitly says deterministic stubbed triage, so no real model-output quality or real external infra is required.
behaviors.test_description: Integration pipeline test wiring real local modules with stub triage output and stub payer statuses; execute full orchestration and assert each stage runs in order with correct shared state into final confirmation.

behaviors.description: The full live intake pipeline (validate, insurance, triage, schedule, confirm) completes for a real patient submission without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: Includes real triage model call in live flow; correctness of automated completion depends on a real model decision feeding downstream scheduling/confirmation. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF (>Integration): integration with stubbed triage would miss live-model outputs that break parser/routing and halt pipeline. Concrete miss above Integration: only live run reveals model returning unexpected urgency/explanation format that disrupts downstream. REJECTION-GATE for Workflow: behavior has one agentic decision point (triage), not multiple sequential model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Agentic end-to-end live test using real patient submission path, real payer integration mode available to test env, and live triage model; assert pipeline reaches confirmation automatically with no operator intervention.
